member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Base.IDutInformation
member.summary: Dut Information

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Base.IDutInformation.Index
member.summary: Unique DUT index. For defined DUTs indexing begins at 0.
member.example: For MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11,12) | DUT | 11 | 12 | |------- |---- |---- | | Index | 0 | 1 |

For INTRADUT MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11, 12) each with two IPs(IPA, IPB) | DUT | 11 | 11_IPA | 11_IPB | 12 | 12_IPA | 12_IPB | |------- |---- |-------- |-------- |---- |-------- |-------- | | Index | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |

members.member:
Unique DUT name. For IP DUT, this is package DUT name with separated by underscore.
For MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11,12)
| DUT | 11 | 12 | |------ |------ |------ | | Name | "11" | "12" |
For INTRADUT MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11, 12) each with two IPs(IPA, IPB) | DUT | 11 | 11_IPA | 11_IPB | 12 | 12_IPA | 12_IPB | |------ |------ |---------- |---------- |------ |---------- |---------- | | Name | "11" | "11_IPA" | "11_IPB" | "12" | "12_IPA" | "12_IPB" |

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Base.IDutInformation.IpName
member.summary: DUT IP block name. For package DUT, this is empty.
member.example: For MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11,12) | DUT | 11 | 12 | |-------- |---- |---- | | IpName | "" | "" |

For INTRADUT MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11, 12) each with two IPs(IPA, IPB) | DUT | 11 | 11_IPA | 11_IPB | 12 | 12_IPA | 12_IPB | |-------- |---- |-------- |-------- |---- |-------- |-------- | | IpName | "" | "IPA" | "IPB" | "" | "IPA" | "IPB" |

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Base.IDutInformation.IpList
member.summary: List of individual IP level DUT information associated with a package level DUT.
member.example: For MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11,12) | DUT | 11 | 12 | |-------- |------- |-------- | | IpList | empty | empty |

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Base.IDutInformation.PackageIndex
member.summary: The of this DUT's package. For package DUTs, this is the same as .
member.example: For MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11,12) | DUT | 11 | 12 | |--------------|----|----| | PackageIndex | 0 | 1 |

For INTRADUT MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11, 12) each with two IPs(IPA, IPB) | DUT | 11 | 11_IPA | 11_IPB | 12 | 12_IPA | 12_IPB | |--------------|----|--------|--------|----|--------|--------| | PackageIndex | 0 | 0 | 0 | 3 | 3 | 3 |

members.member:
The of this DUT's package. For package DUTs, this is the same as
For MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11,12)
| DUT | 11 | 12 | |-------------|------|------| | PackageName | "11" | "12" |
For INTRADUT MDUTx2 test program with package DUTs(11, 12) each with two IPs(IPA, IPB) | DUT | 11 | 11_IPA | 11_IPB | 12 | 12_IPA | 12_IPB | |-------------|------|--------|--------|------|--------|--------| | PackageName | "11" | "11" | "11" | "12" | "12" | "12" |

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.DutConsole.IDutConsoleSdk
member.summary: An interface for writing to the DUT console

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.DutConsole.IDutConsoleSdk.WriteLine(System.String,System.ConsoleColor)
member.summary: Writes a message line to the DUT console
member.param: An optional color for the message, white by default

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Writes a message line to the DUT console. It is expected that the DUT console will print the information on its own line, with its own timestamp.

Notes:
Method returns as quickly as possible, instead of waiting for the console print
Line breaks within text should result in line breaks on the console, but those text lines will have the same timestamp
remarks.example: C# Code Example dutConsole.WriteLine("sample text", ConsoleColor.Red);

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IFunctionalTestBase`1
member.summary: An interface for a functional test

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IFunctionalTestBase`1.PlistName
member.summary: Plist name associated with this functional test

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IFunctionalTestBase`1.ValidateSettingsAsync
member.summary:
Validates the functional test settings, including that:
1. The plist exists 2. The levels and timing test conditions exist (regardless of whether test conditions are automatically applied before executing the plist) 3. Any PatternTriggerMap exists and is valid for the plist 4. All edge counter pins exist, are in one of the plist domains, and have valid modes 5. All mask pins exist and are in one of the plist domains 6. Any start pattern information is valid?
member.remarks: Results in an API call

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IFunctionalTestBase`1.ExecuteAsync
member.summary: Executes the functional test and returns an object that contains results and provides access to capture data

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IFunctionalTestBase`1.SendTriggerToResumePatternAsync
member.summary: Sends a software trigger to resume patgen (if it's still executing)
member.remarks: Results in an API call

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IFunctionalTestBase`1.StopPatgenExecutionAsync
member.summary: Sends a request to stop patgen (if it's still executing)
member.remarks: Results in an API call

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IFunctionalTestBase`1.GetExecutionSequenceAsync
member.summary: Gets the user-defined execution sequence for the PList associated with this functional test object. This is the flattened sequence of patterns in the PList. Information about whether each pattern is skipped and if so, the reason(s), is also included.

member.summary: Options for how to continue plist/patgen execution when the specified maximum number of failures have occurred

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.ExitPListOnFail
member.summary: At any plist burst boundary, if the previous burst(s) fail count exceeds the max fail limit, the plist execution will exit and remaining bursts will not be executed

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.AbortOnFail
member.summary: Plist execution will abort mid pattern if the fails exceed the max fail count.This is not compatible with KeepAlivePLists.

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.ContinueOnFail
member.summary: Plist execution continues (without capturing any additional failures)

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.ExecutionMaxFailBehavior.ReturnOnFail
member.summary: PList will evaluate fails at the pattern boundaries and return through the plist defined PostExec content

The execution behavior when the maximum number of fails are captured

member.summary: Sets the types of data that are captured.
member.param: the type of data to be captured
member.remarks: If is not specified, failing cycle data will not have failing pins. If multiple types are specified and the underlying instrument does not support 2-bit capture, a best effort will be made to emulate any data that was not captured natively. This may incur a performance penalty or be subject to some restrictions.

member.param: the type of data to be captured

list.item: must be called prior to calling this method

member.summary: The pass/fail result from each executed burst.

member.summary: Gets the requested edge counters by pin

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IResult.PinFailureCounts
member.summary: Gets the overall number of failures per pin. Only failing pins will have entries.
member.remarks: Note that this will return the hardware failure counts for each pin. This may be larger than the requested max fail count and may not be deterministic. This can occur due to the non-deterministic nature of abort across domain/slices, resulting in a slice continuing execution and recording pin failures even after a different slice has stopped executing.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IGeneralResult.ReadCaptureDataByPinAsync(HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.CaptureCycleFilter,HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.CaptureType,HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.PinCaptureWindow[])
member.summary: Reads data from capture memory by pin for the specified pins and cycles
member.param: Filter for which captured cycles are returned for each pin
The types of capture data to return
The pins and cycles to return data for
member.exception: Thrown if called after capture data has been invalidated
member.returns: A set of pin data with an element for each pin containing its capture data
member.remarks: Results in an API call In some cases, due to specific hardware implementations, instruments may capture more cycles than the maximum capture counts specified in the capture settings. This API will return all cycles that were captured by the instrument that meet the specified criteria, regardless of the capture settings.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IGeneralResult.ReadCaptureDataByCycleAsync(HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.CaptureCycleFilter,HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.CaptureType,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.UInt32,System.UInt64,System.UInt64)
member.summary: Reads data from capture memory by cycle
member.param: Filter for which captured cycles are returned
The types of capture data to return
The set of pins to return data for. Capture string bit will be in the same order as the requested pins
The index of the pattern from which to begin returning data

The starting cycle index, relative to the pattern index. If there are no captures for the pattern specified by at or beyond the specified cycle index, captures are returned beginning from the first captured cycle of the next pattern.
The number of capture cycles to return
member.exception: Thrown if called after capture data has been invalidated
member.returns: A set of cycle data objects for each captured cycle
member.remarks: Results in an API call. In some cases, due to specific hardware implementations, instruments may capture more cycles than the maximum capture counts specified in the capture settings. This API will return all cycles that were captured by the instrument that meet the specified criteria, regardless of the capture settings.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Functional.IGeneralResult.FreeCaptureDataAsync
member.summary: Frees the capture data stored in TOS associated with this result
member.remarks:
If this method is not called, TOS will not release the capture memory, possibly leading to out-of-memory errors.
Capture memory will not be automatically released when this object is garbage collected.

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.ISdk
member.summary: User SDK APIs

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.ISdk.GetAlarmsAsync
member.summary: Gets the alarms that are currently stored in TOS for the current DUT and clears any that are of a severity that can be cleared by the API.
member.returns: The alarms that are currently stored in TOS
member.remarks: This call will make a call to TOS only if the SDK has received a notification that an alarm has occurred. If an alarm has occurred but the SDK has not yet received an alarm notification then no call will be made (and the alarm will not be cleared). A subsequent call made after the SDK has received the notification will retrieve the alarm from TOS and clear it (if it is of a severity that can be cleared by the API).

member.name: E:HdmtOs.Sdk.ISdk.AlarmRaised
member.summary: An event that is fired when an alarm occurs.
member.remarks: This event is purely for notification and does not clear the alarm. The alarm will still be returned by a call to

member.summary: Creates a general functional test for the specified plist that exposes all setup options and an interface for retrieving arbitrary captured after execution. Settings are exposed via a property on the returned object and are configured with defaults. No settings validation is performed at the time of creation.

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior
member.summary: Behavior describing how memory regions will be cached. If a new memory region overlaps an already existing region in the cache, both will be merged together and the behavior of the new region will be applied to the merged region. This allows to remove a ForceCache region from the cache by doing an overlapping pattern modification with DoNotCache.

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior.DoNotCache
member.summary: memory region will never be cached

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior.MayCache
member.summary: memory region will be cached, but can be removed from the cache if the cache grows too big

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior.ForceCache
member.summary: memory region will always be cached, cannot be removed from the cache if the cache grows too big

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer
member.summary: Container for creating and executing read and write pattern operations

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.AddPinVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.UInt64,System.UInt64,HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior)
member.summary: Creates an object that can read and write pattern pin vectors using an absolute address within the pattern and adds it to the container. The returned object can be used to read or write data for the specified pins only. If any vector in the range is a channel-linked (S) vector then all vectors in the range must be channel-linked.
member.remarks: Setting the to anything other than for any range in the will disable container-level optimizations that combine reads and writes to pattern memory to improve performance. This should only be done in very specific situations that will benefit more from the pattern cache than from optimizing reads/writes across ranges.
member.param: The pins to read/modify; the domain is inferred from the pins, and all pins must be from the same domain
The start address to read/modify within the pattern
The number of vectors to read/modify
Caching behavior for the memory region described by the range (optional, defaults to DoNotCache)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.AddPinVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String,System.UInt64,System.UInt64,HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior)

If any vector in the range is a channel-linked (S) vector then all vectors in the range must be channel-linked.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.AddConvertedInstructionVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.UInt64,System.UInt64,HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior)
member.summary: Creates and immediately validates an object that can write pattern instruction vectors regardless of the current vector types in the range, using an absolute address within the pattern. The vectors will be converted to instruction vectors on write. Currently the existing vectors in the range must be either metadata vectors or already be instruction vectors. This range cannot be used for reading (and will not be populated when as called). Validation consists of verifying the pattern exists and contains the domain and the start address and vector count are valid. Note that the range is not considered dirty immediately. Like the non-converting range, it will be marked dirty when is set. This will also control the order in which the conversion occurs relative to other ranges on write.
member.param: The start address to read/modify within the pattern
The number of vectors to read/modify
Caching behavior for the memory region described by the range (optional, defaults to DoNotCache)
member.remarks: Setting the to anything other than for any range in the will disable container-level optimizations that combine reads and writes to pattern memory to improve performance. This should only be done in very specific situations that will benefit more from the pattern cache than from optimizing reads/writes across ranges.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.AddConvertedInstructionVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.UInt64,System.UInt64,HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior)
member.summary: Creates an object that can write pattern instruction vectors regardless of the current vector types in the range, using a label and offset within the pattern. The vectors will be converted to instruction vectors on write. Currently the existing vectors in the range must be either metadata vectors or already be instruction vectors. This range cannot be used for reading (and will not be populated when as called). Note that the range is not considered dirty immediately. Like the non-converting range, it will be marked dirty when is set. This will also control the order in which the conversion occurs relative to other ranges on write.
member.param: With the offset, the start address to read/modify within the pattern
The offset from the label to read/modify within the pattern
The number of vectors to read/modify
Caching behavior for the memory region described by the range (optional, defaults to DoNotCache)
member.remarks: Setting the to anything other than for any range in the will disable container-level optimizations that combine reads and writes to pattern memory to improve performance. This should only be done in very specific situations that will benefit more from the pattern cache than from optimizing reads/writes across ranges.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.AddConvertedMetadataVectorRange(System.String,System.String,System.UInt64,System.UInt64,HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.CachingBehavior)
member.summary: Creates an object that can write pattern metadata vectors regardless of the current vector types in the range, using an absolute address within the pattern. The vectors will be converted to metadata vectors on write. Currently the existing vectors in the range must be either instruction vectors or already be metadata vectors. This range cannot be used for reading (and will not be populated when as called). Note that the range is considered dirty immediately, since there is no data to set. For ordering of writes, this means the conversion will be added to the end of the list of changes when it is created. This order can be controlled by calling at a later time, after other changes are made.
member.param: The start address to read/modify within the pattern
The number of vectors to read/modify
Caching behavior for the memory region described by the range (optional, defaults to DoNotCache)
member.remarks: Setting the to anything other than for any range in the will disable container-level optimizations that combine reads and writes to pattern memory to improve performance. This should only be done in very specific situations that will benefit more from the pattern cache than from optimizing reads/writes across ranges.

This range cannot be used for reading (and will not be populated when as called). Note that the range is considered dirty immediately, since there is no data to set. For ordering of writes, this means the conversion will be added to the end of the list of changes when it is created. This order can be controlled by calling at a later time, after other changes are made.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.ValidateAllAsync
member.summary: Validates all of the ranges in the container. Validation consists of verifying that the pattern exists and contains the domain, and that the start address and vector count are valid. For ranges of type , a check is also done to ensure the pins belong to the specified domain. The existing vector types are not checked, as this would require reading pattern memory.
member.exception: Thrown if any ranges fail validation, with an inner exception for each range that failed validation.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.ReadAllAsync
member.summary: Populates the data for all created pattern data objects, excluding "converting" ranges (those created by or ). Calling this method results in the container no longer being "dirty." Any data that was set for ranges in the container will be overwritten with the data read from TOS. Converting ranges will not have their data overwritten, but will no longer be considered dirty.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternVectorContainer.WriteAllAsync
member.summary: Writes the data for all created pattern data objects

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternDomainRange
member.summary: A base interface for an object that can read and write vectors within a pattern domain range

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPatternDomainRange.VectorLength
member.summary: The length of data that will be read or written

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPinDataCollection
member.summary: A collection of data for multiple pins

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPinDataCollection.Item(System.String)
member.summary: Gets or sets the data for the specified pin. For a range that does not contain channel-linked vectors, the string consists of one character for each vector in the range. For ranges that contain channel-linked vectors, the string will consist of n characters per vector, where n is the link mode. For example, if the link mode is 4 and there are 3 vectors in the range, the string will consist of 12 characters.
member.remarks: The underscore ("_") character can be used when setting data to indicate that the existing pattern character at that vector (or position in a channel-linked vector) should not be modified on write. The vector corresponding to the underscore character must still be of a valid type for an , meaning it cannot be used to "skip over" other vector types.
member.exception: When getting or setting data for a pin that is not part of this range
When getting the data for a pin if data for the pin for this range has not been previously read or set

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPinDataVectorRange
member.summary: An interface for an object that can read and write pin data vectors within a pattern domain range

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPinDataVectorRange.PinNames
member.summary: The pin names that will be read/written

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPinDataVectorRange.Data
member.summary: The data for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If data set for any pins the data will be modified on write

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPvcVectorRange
member.summary: An interface for an object that can read and write PVC data for vectors within a pattern domain range

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPvcVectorRange.Ctv
member.summary: The CTV values for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If modified, the CTV flags will be changed on write. This value is a single string, with one character for each vector in the range. A '1' character means capture this vector, a '0' character means do not capture this vector.
member.exception: When getting the property value if the CTV data for this range has not been previously read or set

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPvcVectorRange.Mtv
member.summary: The MTV values for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If modified, the MTV flags will be changed on write. This value is a single string, with one character for each vector in the range. A '1' character means mask all pins, a '0' character means mask no pins
member.exception: When getting the property value if the MTV data for this range has not been previously read or set

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPvcVectorRange.Lrpt
member.summary: The LRPT values for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If modified, the LRPT values will be changed on write. This is a set integers, with one value per vector in the range.
member.exception: When getting the property value if the LRPT data for this range has not been previously read or set

member.name: P:HdmtOs.Sdk.PatMod.IPvcVectorRange.Sce
member.summary: The SCE values for the specified pins. This will be populated on read. If the instrument supports SCE, this value will be a single string, with one character for each vector in the range. A '1' character means serial capture is enabled for this vector, a '0' character means it is not enabled. If the instrument does not support SCE, this value will be null.
member.exception: When getting the property value if the SCE data for this range has not been previously read

member.summary: The instruction data. This will be populated on read. If modified, the new instructions will be applied on write.
member.exception: When getting the property value if the instruction data for this range has not been previously read or set

member.summary: Makes a deep copy of this plist and adds it to the container as a new plist. Any referenced plists are copied, with a new name provided by appending the value from to the original name. The new plist(s) will only be created in TOS when is called.

The suffix used to name any nested copied plists by appending it to the original name
member.exception: If data for the source plist or any of its referenced plists have not been read in the container
If a plist with the new name of any copied plist already exists in the container

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.Plist.PlistElementExecutionType.PostExec
member.summary: The plist or pattern is executed after any main patterns or referenced plists. This is the equivalent of the PostExecPat keyword for patterns and the PostExecRefPList keyword for referenced plists.

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionState
member.summary: The state of test condition

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionState.Creating
member.summary: The test condition has been added to the container for creating a new direct test condition. In this state, the test condition is modifiable and after the modification, WriteAllAsync() must be called on its container to create the TC in TOS Test condition is assumed not in TOS

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionState.Unread
member.summary: The test condition has been added to the container for modification after reading, its content needs to be filled in through calling TOS later In this state, the test condition is not modifiable and WriteAllAsync() must be called on its container to retrieve the TC content Test condition is assumed already created in TOS

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionState.Unchanged
member.summary: The test condition has been synced with TOS through either WriteAllAsync() or ReadAllAsync() and no pending modification to sync with TOS

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionState.Modified
member.summary: The test condition has been synced with TOS, but there are more modification since last sync

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionState.Deleting
member.summary: The test condition is marked for deletion after it is synced with TOS In this state, test condition is not modifiable, but it still exists in the container and next WriteAllAsync() call on its container will try to remove this TC from TOS. If removing the tc from TOS succeeds, the tc will also be removed from the test condition container.

member.name: F:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionState.Detached
member.summary: The test condition is removed from the container. In this state, test condition is not modifiable.

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.ITestConditionContainer
member.summary: An interface for a container that can read/write test conditions

remarks.para: Each test conditions in the container maintains its state based on the operations performed on it. Below diagram shows how the state changes under different scenarios.
![](TestConditionStates.png)

list.item: AddExistingTc means calling or on the container to add the test condition
AddNewDirectTc means calling or on the container to add the test condition
Write means calling on the container
Read means calling on the container
Delete means calling on the container
Modify means modifying the content directly on the test condition

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.ITestConditionContainer.AddNewDirectSequentialTestCondition(System.String,HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.TestConditionType)
member.summary: Add a new direct sequential test condition to the container (non-timing) that will be created in TOS when is called later
member.param: The name of the test condition

remarks.para:
Notes: In IntraDUT, when the test condition container belongs to an IP DUT, user provided name must explicitly provide module name and it might be auto scoped and therefore be different from the original name. For example, say the test condition container belongs to 'IPA'
if name 'ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be auto scoped as 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1'
if name 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be used as it is
name 'tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't contain module name
name 'IPB::ModuleX::tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't belong to 'IPA'
name 'IPA::tc1' is considered invalid as it using IP name as its module name

member.param: The type of the test condition
member.returns: The empty test condition object that was added to the container
member.exception: When test condition already exist in the container or test condition name is empty or provided tc name failed the Intradut name validation
When test condition name is null

remarks.para:
Notes: In IntraDUT, when the test condition container belongs to an IP DUT, user provided name must explicitly provide module name and it might be auto scoped and therefore be different from the original name. For example, say the test condition container belongs to 'IPA'

if name 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be used as it is
name 'tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't contain module name
name 'IPB::ModuleX::tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't belong to 'IPA'
name 'IPA::tc1' is considered invalid as it using IP name as its module name

member.remarks: In IntraDUT, when the test condition container belongs to an IP DUT, user provided name might be auto scoped and therefore different from the original name
member.returns: The empty test condition object that was added to the container
member.exception: When test condition already exist in the container or test condition name is empty or provided tc name failed the Intradut name validation
When test condition name is null
This API is not currently implemented

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.ITestConditionContainer.AddExistingSequentialTestCondition(System.String)
member.summary: Add a sequential test condition object to the container so that its content can be populated when is called later
member.param: The name of the test condition

remarks.para:
Notes: In IntraDUT, when the test condition container belongs to an IP DUT, user provided name must explicitly provide module name and it might be auto scoped and therefore be different from the original name. For example, say the test condition container belongs to 'IPA'
if name 'ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be auto scoped as 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1'
if name 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be used as it is
name 'tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't contain module name
name 'IPB::ModuleX::tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't belong to 'IPA'
name 'IPA::tc1' is considered invalid as it using IP name as its module name

member.returns: A to-be-populated test condition that was added to the container
member.exception: When test condition already exist in the container or test condition name is empty or provided tc name failed the Intradut name validation
When test condition name is null

remarks.para:
Notes:
This will only create a placeholder test condition in the container, the test condition content will be filled in through calling
Updating the test condition through will fail if specified type is not aligned with the type retrieved from TOS
Accessing on the test condition before calling will cause InvalidOperationException as it is expected to read first from TOS

if name 'ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be auto scoped as 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1'
if name 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be used as it is

remarks.para:
Notes:
This will only create a placeholder test condition in the container, the test condition content will be filled in through calling
Updating the test condition through will fail if the test condition retrieved from TOS is not a timing test condition
Accessing the test condition before calling will cause InvalidOperationException as it is expected to read first from TOS

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.ITestConditionContainer.ReadAllAsync
member.summary: Reads from TOS to update SDK test conditions that needs to be updated
member.exception: When TOS doesn't return with valid list of test conditions
When test condition read from TOS type is not aligned with SDK test condition
Collection of errors encountered for all problematic tc read

remarks.para:
Notes:
The values of test condition items read back are the original expressions, not the resolved values
Any test condition that is marked for deletion will not be updated through the call
For all test conditions that will be read from TOS, they must already exists. Any non-existing TC will cause no test condition update
Test condition that has any unsaved change (modified, but not called ) will lose the unsaved changes after the call
Each read will clear the previous content of the TC and update according to the content stored in TOS
Read will skip the newly added 'new' direct test conditions through and if they have not being created through yet.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.TestConditions.ITestConditionContainer.Delete(System.String)
member.summary: Mark the test condition as to-be-deleted. The test condition may/may not be immediately removed from the container, depending on its current state.
member.param: The name of the test condition to be removed

remarks.para:
Notes: In IntraDUT, when the test condition container belongs to an IP DUT, user provided name must explicitly provide module name and it might be auto scoped and therefore be different from the original name. For example, say the test condition container belongs to 'IPA'
if name 'ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be auto scoped as 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1'
if name 'IPA::ModuleX::tc1' is provided, it will be used as it is
name 'tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't contain module name
name 'IPB::ModuleX::tc1' is considered invalid as it doesn't belong to 'IPA'
name 'IPA::tc1' is considered invalid as it using IP name as its module name

member.exception: When test condition doesn't exist in the container or provided tc name failed the Intradut name validation

remarks.para:
Notes:
Specified test condition must exist in the container, otherwise this API call will fail
Only direct test condition can be removed through this API, marking normal test condition as to-be-deleted will not fail when this API gets called
Once a test condition is marked as to-be-deleted, and it is a direct test condition, will skip reading this test condition from TOS
If the test condition is just added as 'new' direct tc, and hasn't been created through , this will just remove the test condition from the container
If the test condition is just added as 'existing' tc, and hasn't been populated through , this will just remove the test condition from the container
Once test condition is marked for deletion (Deleting/Detached state), any access to the test condition content will cause InvalidOperationException
Once test condition gets removed from the container (Detached state), user code should release the handle to the test condition to let it garbage collected

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.TrialResultContent
member.summary: Structure that contains the details of trial test result
member.param: Trial result action type expression, which can evaluate to either Next or Exit.
Trial result return type, which can be either ReturnExecutionResult or ReturnUserResult.
Trial result return value if the return type is ReturnUserResult, it will be 0xEBADBAD2 if trial result return type is ReturnExecutionResult.
Trial result property string value, can only be Pass or Fail or Bypass.
Bin name expression for SetBin operation, the value can be empty.
Counter name expressions for IncrementCounters operation. the vector can be empty.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkBinsCtrs.BackupCurrentCountersAsync
member.summary: Backs up the current counters incremented.

remarks.para:
Notes:
Calling HdmtApi::BackupCurrentCounters overwrites any previous backed up counters.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkBinsCtrs.BackupCurrentCountersAsync();
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature BackupCurrentCounters();

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkBinsCtrs.CreateCounterAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Adds new counter based on the information provided.
member.param: The Name of the counter being created -- Mandatory

remarks.para:
Restrictions:
If a similar counter exists, the same counter name cannot be implemented.
If the counter is module specific, the counter name needs to be prepended with the module name followed by double colon e.g. "ModuleName::Counter1". Otherwise, the counter will be created in the base level.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkBinsCtrs.CreateCounterAsync(counterName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature CreateCounter(const std::string& counterName);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkBinsCtrs.GetCountersForLotAsync
member.summary: Returns the lot counter information.
member.returns: Location to store the list of counter information

remarks.para:
Restrictions:
This HdmtApi has to be called only at Lot End (when only lot end flow is running on single thread) or else race condition might happen.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var outputCounterValues = await wrappedSdkBinsCtrs.GetCountersForLotAsync()
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetCountersForLot(std::vector<CounterInfo>& outputCounterValues);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkBinsCtrs.GetCountersForUnitAsync
member.summary: Returns the unit counter information. All TP defined counters are returned with their respective counts per unit
member.returns: Location to store the list of counter information

remarks.example: C# Code Example var outputCountersInfo = await wrappedSdkBinsCtrs.GetCountersForUnitAsync()
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetCountersForUnit(std::vector<CounterInfo>& outputCountersInfo);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkBinsCtrs.RestoreBackedUpCountersAsync
member.summary: Restores backed up counters.

remarks.para:
Notes:
Returns false if HdmtApi::BackupCurrentCounters has not been executed and therefore there is nothing to restore.
HdmtApi::RestoreBackedUpCounters will restore the counters to the values when HdmtApi::BackupCurrentCounters was last called.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkBinsCtrs.RestoreBackedUpCountersAsync()
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature RestoreBackedUpCounters();

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkBinsCtrs.GetBinsForLotAsync
member.summary: Returns the lot bin information.
member.returns: Location to store the bin information.

remarks.para:
Restrictions:
1. This HdmtApi has to be called only at Lot End (when only lot end flow is running on single thread) or else race condition might happen.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var outputBinValues = await wrappedSdkBinsCtrs.GetBinsForLotAsync();
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetBinsForLot(std::vector<BinInfo>& outputBinValues);

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFileMgr
member.summary: Wrappers for HDMT API 1.0 functions in HdmtApiFileMgr.h

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFileMgr.FindFileViaSysCAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Check if a file exists with SysC access privilege and return the result within specified timeout period.
member.param: File name as an input to search its path within search paths
Paths to be searched. The paths should be separated by a semi colon. Use 4 slashes inside quotes to separate directories.
Optional parameter to return within specified time period incase of network issue
member.returns: Found full path including file name

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Find a file from specified search paths with SysC access privilege. TPAD, General PDE usage. File will be searched in the order specified in searchPaths. All the paths specified in searchPaths should be in the view point of System Controller.

Restrictions:
1. System Controller service should be running while this API gets called.
2. Supervisor Service on the Site Controller should be running and have registered to SysC at the point of time this API gets called.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkFileMgr.FindFileViaSysCAsync(fileName, searchedPaths, timeoutInSeconds);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature FindFileViaSysC(const std::string& fileName, const std::string& searchedPaths, std::string& foundPath, int timeoutInSeconds = 0);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFileMgr.IsSysCFileServiceUpAsync
member.summary: Check if System Controller file service is up to be used.
member.returns: Named Tuple(Status, ErrorMessage). Status of the System Controller file service and error message indicating why the service is not up.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
If System Controller file service is up to be used, this API returns true. When service is not up.

Notes:
SysC file service is up means SysC machine is reachable and the file service is ready to be called.
This API tends to be slow when SysC machine is reachable but SysC service is not up.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var result = await wrappedSdkFileMgr.IsSysCFileServiceUpAsync();
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature IsSysCFileServiceUp();

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFileMgr.GetDFFAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the DFF data based on the input visualId and eFuseId.
member.param: Visual Id as an input to search DFF data.
eFuse Id as an input to search DFF data.
Optional parameter to return within specified time period even there is no response from SysC yet

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Used from within a test program when the user test method needs to retrieve missing DFF data for a given unit. TPAD, General PDE usage.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var dffData = await wrappedSdkFileMgr.GetDFFAsync(visualId, eFuseId, timeoutInSeconds);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetDFF(const std::string& visualId, const std::string& eFuseId, std::string& dffData, int timeoutInSeconds = 0);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFileMgr.GetTimestampOnFileViaSysCAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the last-modified and creation timestamps for a file with SysC access privilege and return the result within specified timeout period.
member.param: optional parameter to return within specified time period in case of network issue
member.returns: A named tuple that contains the last modified time of the file, the creation time of the file

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Attempts to access the specified file via SysC and return the last-modified and creation times. The source path should be specified in the view point of System Controller.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var result = await wrappedSdkFileMgr.GetTimestampOnFileViaSysCAsync(sourcePathName, fileName, timeoutInSeconds);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetTimestampOnFileViaSysC(const std::string& sourcePathName, const std::string& fileName, time_t& lastModifiedTime, time_t& creationTime, int timeoutInSeconds = 0);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFileMgr.PullFileToSiteCViaSysCAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Pull a file from source path to Site Controller with SysC access privilege and return the result within specified timeout period.
member.param: Optional parameter to return within specified time period in case the network connection is too slow

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Pull a file from source path to local Site Controller with SysC access privilege. TPAD, General PDE usage. File will be stored on a temp path specified as HDMT_TEMP in ENV file. If this variable is not found, a temp folder will be created (C:/HDMT3/temp). The source path should be specified in the view point of System Controller.

Requirements:
1. System Controller service should be running while this API gets called.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var destinationPath = await wrappedSdkFileMgr.PullFileToSiteCViaSysCAsync(sourcePathName, fileName, destinationPath, timeoutInSeconds);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature PullFileToSiteCViaSysC(const std::string& sourcePathName, const std::string& fileName, std::string& destinationPath, int timeoutInSeconds = 0);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFileMgr.PushFileViaSysCAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: push a file to destination path with SysC access privilege and return the result within specified timeout period.
member.param: Optional parameter to return within specified time period in case the network connection is too slow

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Push a file to destination path with SysC access privilege. TPAD, General PDE usage. The destination path should be specified in the view point of System Controller.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkFileMgr.PushFileViaSysCAsync(sourcePathName, fileName, destinationPath, timeoutInSeconds);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature PushFileViaSysC(const std::string& sourcePathName, const std::string& fileName, const std::string& destinationPath, int timeoutInSeconds = 0);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkFunctional.CheckIfPlistExistsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the plist exists or not and returns a true/false status.
member.returns: true if plist is found | false if plist is not found

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Verifies if the plist exists or not and returns a true/false result

Notes:
plistName must be a valid plist.

TODO: HdmtApi 1.0 Restrictions

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPattern.GetVectorAddressForLabelAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get the vector address of a given label
member.returns: vector address that corresponds to the given pattern label name

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Return the vector addresses for a given pattern/domain. The patternLabelName must be an exact match. The API will return false if the label name was not found for the given pattern/domain If the API is called with an address input value where there is already a label exists, then regardless of the value of the bool, the API at its current implementation will always return that specific label
Example:
Vector CTV Pins.. anyFail? 1 - HL01XZ - Label1:: 2 Y HL01XZ - 3 Y HL01XZ - Label2:: 4 HL01XZ Y 5 Y HL01XZ Y 6 Y HL01XZ - ` Label3:: 7 Y HL01XZ - 8 Y HL01XZ - 9 Y HL01XZ -
For vector address 4, since there is already a label either search direction will return Label2 For vector address 3, searchUpForLabel = true will return Label1 and searchUpForLabel = false will return Label2

Notes:
patternName must be a valid patternName name.
label must be valid.
domain name must be valid

Restrictions:
searchUpForLabel = true , API returns the immediate label preceding the address location provided by user
searchUpForLabel = false , API returns the immediate label succeeding the address location provided by user
remarks.example: C# Code Example var vectorAddress = await wrappedSdkPattern.GetVectorAddressForLabelAsync(patternName, patternLabelName, domainName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature HdmtApi::GetVectorAddressForLabel(PatternName, PatternLabel, DomainName, vectorAddress)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPattern.GetPatternLabelsAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Get Pattern labels by sub string per domain
member.param: If true, patternLabelName is matched as a standard RegEx. Otherwise, it is matched as a substring with * as a wildcard (optional, default is false).
member.returns: result of Pattern labels result information

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Get a matching list of labels and their resolved addresses for a given pattern/domain. The patternLabelName parameter can be an Exact match or a regular expression syntax

Notes:
patternName must be a valid patternName name.
label must be valid.
offset should not cause out of bounds on the pattern address.
returns all labels in a pattern when "patternLabelName" is an empty string.
Refer https://www.w3schools.com/jsref/jsref_obj_regexp.asp for more information about the behavior when parseByStandardRegEx is enabled and the regEx engine used.
remarks.example:
C# Code Example
var patternLabelResults = await wrappedSdkPattern.GetPatternLabelsAsync(patternName, patternLabelName, domainName, parseByStandardRegEx:true);

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetPatternLabels(const std::string& patternName, const std::string& patternLabelName, const std::string& domainName, PatternLabelsVector& OutputPatternLabels, bool parseByStandardRegEx = false);

member.summary: Reload given pattern.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Reload the pattern whose name is specified in the argument from the pattern object source.

Notes:
patternName must be a valid patternName name.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkPattern.ReloadPatternAsync(patternName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature ReloadPattern(const std::string& patternName);

members.member:
Inserts instructions vectors on a given patter Name of the pattern Domain name Map containing the list of instructions to be inserted on a given vector address (vector address is the key) void C++ API returned false///
Detailed Description:
Inserts instruction vectors on a given pattern
Notes:
1. If the API returns false, the error message can be retrieved from HdmtApi::GetLastHdmtApiStatus()
2. If an instruction is added before an instruction that uses a label (Call, JMP) the relative address will be updated
3. It is valid to insert instruction(s) on vector address 0, but use it at your own risk
Restrictions:
1. Must be a valid pattern name
2. Must be a main pattern (cannot be used for subroutine patterns)
3. The domain must exist based on the given pattern
4. Must be a valid instruction
5. Must be a valid vector address
C# Code Example await wrappedSdkPattern.InsertInstructionVectorsAsync(patternName, domainName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature InsertInstructionVectors(const std::string& patternName, const std::string& domainName, const::std::map<size_t, InstructionData>& addressToInstructionMap);

Unload given pattern. pattern name to unload void C++ API returned false///
Detailed Description:
Pattern will unload if it is not being used by any Plist. Pattern will not unload a subroutine pattern type. Pattern will not unload a KeepAlive pattern if it is executing - UnlockKeepAlivePattern in RemovePListOption will check if isKeepAliveExecuting.Pattern will unload if it isn't executing
Notes:
1. Valid values required
- patternName must be a valid patternName name.
- pattern can't be a subroutine type.
C# Code Example await wrappedSdkPattern.UnloadPatternAsync(patternName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature UnloadPattern(const std::string& patternName);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPattern.GetLabelInPatternSearchByAddressAsync(System.String,System.String,System.UInt64,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Pattern label from the provided address /per domain
member.param: vector address to search for label

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Get a matching label from provided pattern address for a given pattern/domain. If it failed to get a label on given address, it will continue to look for the valid label name on preceding or succeeding address depending on search direction provided. If the pattern label not found within the pattern address boundary, it will return false

Notes:
1.Valid values required
- patternName must be a valid patternName name.
- domainName must be valid domain name.
- pattern address should not cause out of bounds on the pattern address
- search direction whether to search for the preceding or succeeding address if no label found on the given address
remarks.example: C# Code Example var ouputPatternLabel = await wrappedSdkPattern.GetLabelInPatternSearchByAddressAsync(patternName, domainName, searchAtAddress, searchUpForLabel);

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetLabelInPatternSearchByAddress(const std::string& patternName, const std::string& domainName, const size_t searchAtAddress, bool searchUpForLabel, PatternLabel& outputPatternLabel);

member.summary: Insert or Replace Label in a pattern.
member.param: Address where the label is deleted
Label name that is inserted or that replaces the old label

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Add a new label at the given address or replace the label at input address with input label name. Constraints on label name apply.

l Notes:
1.Valid values required
- patternName must be a valid patternName name.
- domainName must be valid domain name.

Restrictions:

2. A Label name can start with an alphabet or an underscore. Rest of the name can have only alphabet, digit or an underscore.

member.summary: Directly modifies the hardware with the pin, attributes and values provided.
member.param: pin or pin group to set values
list of attributes to set values

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Takes user input of pin, attribute names and attribute values and sets the values on the corresponding hardware.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkPins.SetPinAttributeValuesAsync(pinName:"DPS_LC_00", attributeNames, attributeValues);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature SetPinAttributeValues(const std::string& pinName, const std::vector<std::string>& attributeNames, const std::vector<std::string>& attributeValues);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPins.GetPinAttributeValuesAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Directly reads the hardware pin and attribute values provided.
member.param: list of attributes to get values

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Takes user input of pin (or a pin group containing one pin) and attribute names and returns the attribute values via the parameter OutputPinAttributeValues.

Notes:
pinName must be a valid pin name.
attributeNames must be a list of valid attributes for the resource type of the pinName.

Restrictions:
For attribute values that specify a real number, the value returned is in the base unit form. For example, not mV or mA.
This API is not supported for the DPS 'PowerScale' attribute when in a Multi-zone thermal (MZT) environment.
See associated instrument .dat files for more information or for per-attribute restrictions.
Pin names as described in the pin file but not pin group containing more than one pin are supported as the 1st input parameter.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var attributeValues = await wrappedSdkPins.GetPinAttributeValuesAsync("",{"",""});
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetPinAttributeValues(const std::string& pinName, const std::vector<std::string>& attributeNames, std::vector<std::string>& OutputPinAttributeValues);

member.returns: Value Tuple(Result, AdditionalErrorInfo). If there is a validation failure, Result will be FALSE and AdditionalErrorInfo will contain the validation failure reason

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Verifies the attribute value for a given attribute and pin. If the attribute value is invalid (outputVerifyResult is false), failure information will be returned through additionalErrorInfo.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var validationResults = await wrappedSdkPins.VerifyPinAttributeValueAsync(pinName:"HDDPS_VLC_00", attributeName:"IClampHi", attributeValue:"20e-6");

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPins.GetUeiClientTypeAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Get UEI ID and ID type for UEI pin or pin group.
member.returns: Named tuple(ClientTypeID, ClientType) containing UEI type ID for the corresponding UEI device connected to the specified pinName, User readable UEI type for the corresponding UEI device connected to the specified pinName

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Queries UEI device connected to the specified pin for its UEI type ID. Returns the UEI ID as an unsigned int and as a user readable string.

Notes:
If no physical UEI device is detected the 'UeiVirtualClientId' UEI type ID will be returned (if UEI loopbacks are installed), else will return false.
Currently-known UEI IDs are listed below. This list may not be complete. See "UEI Client IDs.docx" in ACMS for full list.
If new clients are created after a given TOS release, that TOS will not have their name to lookup. In that case, this API will return false, clientTypeID set to 0, and clientType set to empty.
Name: ID: Notes:
"UeiVirtualClientId" 62C583D0 TOS regression and diags only
"UeiSimClientId" 582AD113 TOS sim only
"UeiDtcClientId" 56D4CCFC
"UeiTccx1ClientId" F46D0F6D
"UeiTccx18ClientId" 4592BE39
"UeiIntecClientId" 9BC86DFA
"UeiTccClientId" 843C0CD1
"UeiTccHbiClientId" 2EB9860B
"UeiTcc2.6ClientId" EA134398
"UeiTcc5.0ClientId" 902FF066 "iDragon"
remarks.example: C# Code Example var result = wrappedSdkPins.GetUeiClientTypeAsync(pinName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetUeiClientType(const std::string& pinName, unsigned int& clientTypeID, std::string& clientType);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPins.ReadTdauVIAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Read parametric voltage/current data of a TDAU pin or pin group.
member.returns: Set of parametric voltage/current data of a TDAU pin or pin group

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Returns the parametric voltage/current data of a TDAU pin or pin group. Result 'TdauVIParametricData' returned contains a latest temperature reading, voltage, current, base current leakage, emitter current leakage for each of ie1, ie2, ie3.

Notes:
When 'Read VI' mode is enabled it will take the TDAU (entire TDAU, all 4 channels) out of Auto Mode and put it into the read VI mode, it will also interrupt the UEI streaming.

Restrictions:
This HDMT API can only be used on correctly configured TDAU channel.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var data = wrappedSdkPins.ReadTdauVIAsync(pinName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature ReadTdauVI(const std::string& pinName, std::vector<TdauVIParametricData>& data);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPins.GetPinPowerScaleValueAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Obtain the MasterVoltageRailIndex and PowerScale for a DPS pin.
member.param: Thermal domain name to obtain data from. Used for MZT and ignored in non-MZT.
member.returns: Vector of dataType RailPowerScaleValue containing MasterVoltageRailIndex and PowerScale for all the ganged rails which make up the DPS pin.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Returns a vector of the MasterVoltageRailIndex/PowerScale for all rails on a DPS pin.
Result 'powerScaleValues' returned contains the masterRailSlotIndex, slotIndex, channelNumber, masterVoltageRailIndex, powerScale and sumScale of all rails for the pin.

Notes:
If Multi-Zone thermal is used in the test program, then the thermalDomainName must be a valid thermal zone otherwise the API will fail, else thermalDomainName is ignored.
Ganged pin will return powerScaleValues for each rail in the ganged pin.

Restrictions:
This HDMT API can only be used on correctly configured DPS pin.
If Multi-Zone thermal is used in the test program, then the thermalDomainName must be valid.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var powerScaleValues = await wrappedSdkPins.GetPinPowerScaleValueAsync("Pin1", "ThermalDomain1");
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetPinPowerScaleValue(const std::string& dpsPinName, const std::string& thermalDomainName, std::vector<RailPowerScaleValue>& powerScaleValues);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPins.GetPowerScaleValuesAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Obtain the MasterVoltageRailIndex and PowerScale for all rails.
member.param: Thermal domain name to obtain data from. Used for Multi-Zone thermal (MZT).
member.returns: VectorVector of dataType RailPowerScaleValue containing MasterVoltageRailIndex and PowerScale for all the ganged rails which make up the DPS pin

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
ReturnsReturns the MasterVoltageRailIndex and PowerScale for all DPS pins.
Result 'PowerScaleValues' returned contains the masterRailSlotIndex, slotIndex, channelNumber, masterVoltageRailIndex, and powerScale for all DPS pins.

Notes:
In MZT, thermalDomainName must be a valid thermal zone otherwise API will fail. In non-MZT it is ignored.
Returns all the non-zero power scales for each pin.
Ganged pin will return powerScaleValues for each rail in the ganged pin.

Restrictions:
If Multi-Zone thermal is used in the Test Program, then the thermalDomainName must be valid.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var allPowerScaleValues = await wrappedSdkPins.GetPowerScaleValuesAsync("ThermalDomain1");
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetPowerScaleValues(const std::string& thermalDomainName, std::vector<RailPowerScaleValue>& allPowerScaleValues);

member.summary: Get all the loaded plist file names with their full paths.

Get all the loaded plist file names with their full paths.

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetLoadedPListFilePaths(std::vector<std::string>& outputFullPlistPaths);

Get all pattern list names.

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetAllPlists(std::vector<std::string>& outputAllPlistNames);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPlist.LoadPListFileAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Load new patterns from the plist file to the tester.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Load new patterns from the plist file to the tester. PList File should be present in the HDST_PLIST_PATH path of the loaded ENV file or the ENV file of a loaded test plan. PinIObjs for the patterns in the plist are expected to be in the HDST_PAT_PATH of the ENV file. If the patterns are already loaded, then API just returns true. Else it tries to load the new patterns for that plist. Output is plistStatus contains a plist name and whether that plist is loaded/skipped/error.

Restrictions:
1. PList File Name specified should be just a file name that exists in one of the directories of HDST_PLIST_PATH of the ENV File that is loaded or that of a loaded test plan.
2. If the plist is not found in the loaded ENV file, the ENV files of all loaded test plans will be searched in alphabetical order (of test plan name) until the plist is found.
3. If the test plan is specified as part of the plist file name, only the HDST_PLIST_PATH of the ENV file in the test plan that is specified will be searched.
4. Name of the file should either end in .plist extension or it can be empty extension.
5. If there are multiple PLists with same name present in different directories, the plist file found in the first directory of the HDST_PLIST_PATH is used by the API.
6. PinObjs have to be in HDST_PAT_PATH.
7. Only Version 5.0 plist grammar is supported.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var plistStatus = await wrappedSdkPlist.LoadPListFileAsync(plistFileName)
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature LoadPListFile(const std::string& plistFileName, std::map<std::string, std::string>& plistStatus);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPlist.UnloadPListAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Removes the plist elements(both patterns and nested PLists) options on the plist and plist itself.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Unloads all the patterns and plist child elements in the plist. If the patterns are not referred by any other plist the patterns are deleted from the pattern memory. If the plist options specified are pre pattern, post pattern, pre burst, post burst and the pattern specified as the option is not referred by any other plist, the pattern is removed from the pattern memory of the tester. All other options are deleted for this plist.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkPlist.UnloadPListAsync(plistName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature UnloadPList(const std::string& plistName);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkPlist.MakePListGroupAvailableAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Makes all the PLists (both patterns and nested PLists) of a given group available to execute.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Makes all the PLists that are part of the given group available to execute. PList group name should be a valid group present in the plist-xml file.
If the available memory is not sufficient, eviction of PLists based on the selected eviction strategy will be triggered to make room for the newly loaded PLists.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkPlist.MakePListGroupAvailableAsync(groupName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature MakePListGroupAvailable(const std::string& groupName);

member.name: T:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkSensorCollection
member.summary: Wrappers for HDMT API 1.0 functions in HdmtApiSensorCollection.h

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkSensorCollection.DeleteSensorCollectionAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Deletes the specified 'collectionName'.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Deletes the specified 'collectionName' and releases the memory associated with this collection. Once this API is called, the collection data can no longer be retrieved and the collection is unknown.

Notes:
See example call order on StartSensorCollection()
Upon test program unload, any active collections will be stopped and deleted.
Once a collection is started with HdmtApi::StartSensorCollection(), this API can be called without 1st calling HdmtApi::StopSensorCollection() which is only useful while using HdmtApi::GetStreamingSensorCollectionData().
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkSensorCollection.DeleteSensorCollectionAsync(collectionName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature DeleteSensorCollection(const std::string& collectionName);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkSensorCollection.GetStreamingSensorCollectionDataAsync(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Retrieves all the sensor data from specified 'collectionName'.
member.param: Pause/stop Data Streaming for the duration when TOS puts a DUT on hold |
member.returns: collection start time in milliseconds, milliseconds interval of the samples, name of the pins with data in 'outputData', measurement types returned, "OutputData">returned data for pins representing the associated 'type' and 'pin'., markers with time offset in milliseconds from start time of collection

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Retrieves all of the pins the sensor data including marker data from specified 'collectionName'. All data will be time aligned from the first entry, this time value is returned in 'startTime'. All pin data in the collection will be time aligned, so that all channels return the same data count if they have the same sample rate.

Notes:
- See example call order on StartSensorCollection()
- Upon test program unload, any active collections will be stopped and deleted.
- See note about special sensor values that may be returned for TDAU and Serial Capture/DTS on GetSensorCollectionData() above.
- Output parameter 'pins' may contain duplicate names, so can be associated with subsequent sensor types in 'types' for pins that have multiple types, like "Voltage", "Current" for DPS. See the example return data in the code example above for more info..
- Once this API is called, the internal cache for 'outputData' and 'outputMarker' will be cleared, so next call to this API will contain fresh data.
- When this API is called back-to-back, a new set of data is returned, so this is suited for live streaming from the collection.
- 'pins', 'types', 'outputData' should have the same number of entries. Each list entry in the outputData should correspond to one associated type entry and pin entry at the same index.
- See note on GetSensorCollectionData() regarding special sensor values that may be returned for TDAU and Serial Capture/DTS.

Restrictions:
- This API is intended for live streaming, to be called between StartSensorCollection() and StopSensorCollection() to retrieve data on-the-fly.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var getStreamingSensorCollectionDataResult = await wrappedSdkSensorCollection.GetStreamingSensorCollectionData(collectionName, removeOnHoldDutSamples)

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetStreamingSensorCollectionData(const std::string& collectionName, unsigned long long& startTime, int& sampleRate, std::list<std::string>& pins, std::list<std::string>& types, std::list<std::vector<double> >& outputData, std::vector<std::pair<unsigned int, std::string> >& outputMarkers, const bool removeOnHoldDutSamples = true);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkSensorCollection.MarkSensorCollectionAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Set a time stamped marker in the specified 'collectionName' with specified 'marker'.
member.param: markers with time offset in milliseconds from start time of collection

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Set a time stamped marker in the specified 'collectionName' with specified 'marker'. Sets a time stamped marker in the specified 'collectionName' so that user can correlate the marker to a particular sensor value when sensor data is retrieved.The time stamp saved is defined as the offset in milliseconds from start time of collection.

Notes:
See example call order on StartSensorCollection()
Upon test program unload, any active collections will be stopped and deleted.

Restrictions:
- This API can be called only after sensor collection is started by calling HdmtApi::StartSensorCollection(), otherwise there is no collection where to set a marker.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkSensorCollection.MarkSensorCollectionAsync(collectionName, marker);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature MarkSensorCollection(const std::string& collectionName, const std::string& marker);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkSensorCollection.StartSensorCollectionAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Int32)
member.summary: Starts a new collection with specified name 'collectionName' with data from specified 'pins'.
member.param: User specified name of this collection
Pins to collect measurement data on

Ratio to sample at a rate of 4ms intervals. Valid range is 1 to 250. The slowest sample rate at 250 is 1000ms, and the fastest sample rate at 1 is 4ms. Value of 1 means all samples, 2 is every other, 3 is every third,...|

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Creates a new sensor collection named 'collectionName' associated with specified resources only with the DUT context in which this API was called. The sensor collection will immediately begin caching sensor data for specified 'pins'.

Restrictions:
- This HDMT API does not support pin groups as input for the parameter 'pins'.
- If including SerialCapture pins, HdmtApi::ApplySerialCaptureGroupMap() must be called first with a group that includes the associated pins.
- If streaming is already enabled and a new SCGM is applied that does not contain the SC pins that are streaming, they will not post new values in SensorCollection. Also, see GetSensorCollectionData() for default values.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkSensorCollection.StartSensorCollectionAsync(collectionName, pins, sampleRatio);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature StartSensorCollection(const std::string& collectionName, const std::vector<std::string>& pins, int sampleRatio);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkSensorCollection.StopSensorCollectionAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Stop caching sensor data for the specified 'collectionName'.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Stop caching sensor data for the specified 'collectionName'. The data will then be available including samples up until the time this API was called.The collection data will remain available until HdmtApi::DeleteSensorCollection() is called for the test program is unloaded.No new sensor data will be added to the collection once it is stopped.

Notes:
- See example call order on StartSensorCollection()
- Upon test program unload, any active collections will be stopped and deleted.
- Once HdmtApi::StopSensorCollection() is called, the user can retrieve the data as many times as needed via HdmtApi::GetSensorCollectionData() or HdmtApi::GetSensorCollectionMarkers() until HdmtApi::DeleteSensorCollection() is called or the test program is unloaded.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkSensorCollection.StopSensorCollectionAsync(collectionName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature StopSensorCollection(const std::string& collectionName);

Check if smart test condition is enabled.

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature IsSmartTCEnabled(bool& trueIfSmartTCIsEnabled)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestConditions.IsValidToApplyAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Takes user input of TestCondition name and checks if it is valid to apply it to hardware, based on patgen execution status during KeepAlive bursts.
member.param: TestCondition name to apply to hardware
member.returns: Bool true:Patgen execution status allows timing test conditions to be applied | false:Patgen execution status allows timing test conditions to not be applied

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Takes user input of TestCondition name and checks if it is valid to apply it to hardware, based on patgen execution status during KeepAlive bursts. Caution: This API only works for checking patgen status while the system is in a KeepAlive burst, not any normal burst. If the system is only in a normal burst, calling this api should be avoided because it will always return true. During normal bursts, user code should not rely on this method as an indication that applying test conditions is allowed.

Notes:
testConditionName must be a valid TestCondition name.
If an invalid TestCondition is provided, then false is returned and the HdmtApiStatus is updated to reflect the error.
If the system is only in a normal burst, this method will always return true.
remarks.example: C# Code Example bool isValidToApply = await wrappedSdkTestConditions.IsValidToApplyAsync(testConditionName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature IsValidToApply(const std::string& testConditionName)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestConditions.CheckIfTestConditionExistsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the test condition exists or not and returns a true/false status.
member.returns: true-API executed without error / false-API executed with error

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Verifies if the plist exists or not and returns a true/false result - if returned as true then test condition is found. - if returned as false, then test condition is not found.
remarks.example: C++ Original HdmtApi Signature CheckIfTestConditionExists(const std::string& nameOfTestCondition, bool& trueIfTestConditionExists)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestConditions.ValidatePatternTriggerMapAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Validates the available trigger pattern map against the provided plist pattern trigger table.
member.param: Name of the trigger map to be validated
Name of the pattern list to be executed

list.item: True: If there are valid trigger types and trigger name provided in the corresponding plist file(.pxr) and the pattern trigger map(.ptm)

True: If the validation is bypass.This happen when there are no valid available trigger types and trigger names provided in the corresponding plist file(.pxr) even though there are valid trigger map in .ptm file.
False: If during the validation process, it cannot find the current trigger name or trigger types in the trigger map file (.ptm).

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Validates the supplied PatternTriggerMap contents in the Pattern Trigger Map(.ptm) file against the provided plist trigger names available in the pattern trigger table section of.pxr file. The API takes the plist input name and finds the corresponding trigger names allocated based on their trigger types.Then, it does the validation cross-check with the available loaded trigger map. During the check, the API make sure that there are any valid trigger names being allocated based on their specified trigger types. If there aren't any valid specified trigger types, then it will bypass the validation process. Otherwise, it is valid and gets all their trigger names and their corresponding test conditions.

Notes: Valid Values Required:
The value of the supplied triggerMapName parameter must be a valid PatternTriggerMap name in the.ptm file in the loaded TPL content.
The value of the supplied plistName parameter must be a valid plist and a plist which corresponds to a.pxr file with the same trigger definitions.
ValidatePatternTriggerMap HdmtApi should be called before calling ApplyPatternTriggerMap HdmtApi.

Restrictions:
Currently only the LevelsTC trigger type is valid. If this trigger type is not being provided in the corresponding plist file (.pxr), it will bypass the validation.
remarks.example: C# Code Example bool result = await wrappedSdkTestConditions.ValidatePatternTriggerMapAsync(triggerMapName, plistName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature ValidatePatternTriggerMap(triggerMapName, plistName);

Valid values required:

The value of the supplied plistName parameter must be a valid plist and a plist which corresponds to a.pxr file with the same trigger definitions.
ValidatePatternTriggerMap HdmtApi should be called before calling ApplyPatternTriggerMap HdmtApi.

Restrictions:
Currently only the LevelsTC trigger type is valid. If this trigger type is not being provided in the corresponding plist file (.pxr), it will bypass the validation.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkTestConditions.ApplyPatternTriggerMapAsync(triggerMapName, plistName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature ApplyPatternTriggerMap(triggerMapName, plistName);
