member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestConditions.SetTestConditionVariableValueAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Allows direct modification of a SpecSet variable used by a test condition without knowledge of SpecSet selector

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Allows direct modification of a SpecSet variable used by a test condition without knowledge of SpecSet selector. Note that this will impact any Test Condition that also uses that same SpecSet variable/selector combination.

Notes:
testConditionName must be a valid TestCondition name.
variableName must be a valid SpecSet Variable name associated with testConditionName.
variableValue must be a valid value type supported by the attribute the SpecSet variable is assigned to.

Restrictions:
For variable values that specify a real number, specify the value as the base voltage or base current. For example, not mV or mA.
For variable values that specify a discrete setting such as OPMode, specify the specific mode. For example, VSIM not vsim.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkTestConditions.SetTestConditionVariableValueAsync(testConditionName, variableName, variableValue);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature HdmtApi::SetTestConditionVariableValue(_LevelTC, VIH, value)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestConditions.GetTestConditionItemValueAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Allows retrieval of a level or timing block attribute value regardless of the use of a specification set variable.
member.returns: Tuple Type of (string ItemValue, string ItemType) to represent the Test Condition Item Value

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Allows retrieval of a level or timing block attribute value regardless of the use of a specification set variable.

Notes:
testConditionName must be a valid TestCondition name.
pinName can be empty for reading a spec set variable.
pinName must be a Pin or Pin Group that is used within the Level/Timing Block.
pinName may be a Pin that is only defined within a Pin Group used within the Level/Timing Block. In this case the value set on the parent Pin Group is returned.
For Level Blocks the Pin/Pin Group name specified by pinName is searched for in reverse order (i.e. last applied).
itemName must be a valid TestCondition Attribute name associated with testConditionName and Pin Name.

Restrictions:
Currently does not support getting attribute values from Thermal Control or Thermal Measure test conditions.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var testConditionAttributeValue = await wrappedSdkTestConditions.GetTestConditionItemValueAsync(testConditionName, pinName, itemName)
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature HdmtApi::GetTestConditionItemValue(sTestConditionName, sPinName, sAttributeName, tcValue, sItemType)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestConditions.GetTestConditionComponentsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Get test condition information including test condition group, specification set, selector, block name, and block type.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Populates the passed in TCInfo object with information about the given test condition. The fields of the output struct include the group, specification set, selector, block name, and block type of the test condition. TCInfo::blockType will be set to one of the following values: Levels, DynamicGangControl, Timing, ThermalMeasure, ThermalControl, Trigger.

Restrictions:
1. Valid values required:
- testConditionName must be a valid test condition name.
- An empty string will be returned for fields that are not applicable for that test condition.

Notes:
TCInfo::level, TCInfo::timing and TCInfo::trigger are deprecated. Use TCInfo::blockName and TCInfo::blockType instead to determine the block name and type of the test condition.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var OutputTCInfo = await wrappedSdkTestConditions.GetTestConditionComponentsAsync(testConditionName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetTestConditionComponents(const std::string& testConditionName, TCInfo& OutputTCInfo);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestConditions.SetSpecificationSetVariableValueAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Modifies the given SpecificationSet variable value related to the specified TestCondition.
member.param: value of the variable to change

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Modifies the given SpecificationSet variable value related to the specified TestCondition.

Notes:
1. Valid values required
- specSetName must be a valid SpecificationSet name.
- selectorName must be a valid SpecificationSet selector name.
- variableName must be a valid SpecificationSet variable name associated with testConditionName.
2. Global update
- A SpecificationSet variable modification will force a modification to all TestConditions which are related to that SpecificationSet.

Restrictions:
1. For variable values that specify a real number, specify the value as the base voltage or base current. For example, not mV or mA.
2. For attribute values that specify a discrete setting such as OPMode, specify the specific mode. For example, VSIM not vsim.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkTestConditions.SetSpecificationSetVariableValueAsync(specSetName, selectorName, variableName, expressionValue);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature SetSpecificationSetVariableValue(const std::string& specSetName, const std::string& selectorName, const std::string& variableName, const std::string& expressionValue);

Notes:

- variableName must be a valid SpecificationSet variable name associated with testConditionName.

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetExtendedSpecificationSetVariable(const std::string& specSetName, const std::string& selectorName,const std::string& variableName, ExpressionDataStruct& outputVariableData, bool includeInherited = true);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.GetRcHwSyncTimerAsync
member.summary: Get the Resource Card's FPGA microsecond sync timer value.
member.returns: The current 32bit us timer value read from the RC FPGA. The timer this value is read from will rollover after ~71.5 minutes.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Reads a 32bit register on the RC FPGA that holds a microsecond timer value used in synchronizing instruments that communicate with the RC card.

Notes:
1. RCTC3 and HBITC timer is reset on TOS init and ScopeShot start, and is used to synchronize sensor events across RC, TDAU, UEI, and DPS cards for ScopeShot, SensorStreaming, and UEI.
2. This timer is stored in a 32-bit register and will therefore loop after ~71.5 minutes.
3. This is the same timer exposed to UEI with streamable value TC_STREAMABLE_VAL_TC_TIME_FPGA_US.

Restrictions:
1. RCTC2 uses a timer with only 10us granularity. The return time is represented in microseconds, but will only increment by 10us in 10us intervals.
2. RCTC2 does not have any way of resetting its timer.It will increment till the time rolls over.It uses a 32-bit value and will loop after ~71.5 minutes.
3. RCTC2 timer does not synchronize with ScopeShot or SensorCollection.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var result = await wrappedSdkTester.GetRcHwSyncTimerAsync();
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetRcHwSyncTimer(uint32_t& syncTimerUs)

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.SetSerialCaptureGroupFilterAlarmEnableStateAsync(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Enables or disables issuing Error alarms if Serial Capture (SC) / DTS readings are outside of a serial capture group's min/max filter. SC filter Error alarms are disabled by default
member.param: Set true to enable issuing alarms for filtered SC readings, false to disable.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Enables or disables issuing Error alarms if serial capture readings are outside of a serial capture group's min/max filter. Serial capture group settings are configured through.scgm files.See HdmtApi::ApplySerialCaptureGroupMap() and OTPL keyword SerialCaptureGroupMap for more details. To avoid flooding the system with potential SC alarms, SC Error alarms are currently limited to 1 alarm per DUT per second.

Notes: Preconditions for the alarm:
These alarms are only allowed on RCTC3 systems. (This API will return false if not on a tester with an RCTC3).
UEI must be actively streaming.
This API must be called with enable=true. This is global for all DUTs and SC groups.
An .scgm file (SerialCaptureGroupMap) must have been loaded. See note above.
Serial Capture (DTS) sensors must be actively read through pattern content.
An SC reading that was assigned to an SC group must exceed its group's configured min/max filter value.See VirtualScs.dat for details regarding SC group settings (FilterMin, FilterMax, LinearCoefficient, ConstantCoefficient).

Restrictions:
This API is only supported on Testers which contain an RCTC3. If the tester does not contain a supported RC instrument this API will return false.
Due to the nature of the thermal subsystem alarm polling, alarms may be delayed by ~100ms, or more if there are back-to-back SC Error alarms for the same DUT.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var result = await wrappedSdkTester.SetSerialCaptureGroupFilterAlarmEnableStateAsync(enable);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature SetSerialCaptureGroupFilterAlarmEnableState(bool enable);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.SetTesterSafeStateAsync
member.summary: Set Tester To Safe state.

remarks.para:

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature SetTesterSafeState();

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature WriteTIUBoardCounterToEEPROM(const unsigned int EEPROMIndex, const unsigned int CounterIndex, const unsigned int EEPROMSiteIndex, unsigned int& CounterValue, int tiuIndex = -1);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.ExecuteAndSaveTDRToFileAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: TDR cal measures the propagation time of the TIU trace to the DUT so that the tester can make adjustments to timings to be accurate at the DUT. The results are saved to a file if it's true. Otherwise, output file isn't generated for TDR Cal failures.
member.param: If the list of ignore pins is empty, it will use all the pins that belong to the pin group. Otherwise, it will ignore the pins listed
File name with full path to save the TDR cal results
member.returns: A collection of TDRCalFailResult for the failing pins. If the list is empty, all pins passed

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
TDR cal measures the propagation time(the amount of time for an electrical signal to travel across a trace on the TIU) of the TIU trace to the DUT so that the tester can make adjustments to timings to be accurate at the DUT. If the list of ignore pins is empty, it will use all the pins that belong to the pin group. Otherwise, it will ignore the pins listed. For the passing pins, it will read the measurement value of each unique channel id from the pin group name and write the TDR results to the file name specified. For the failing pins, no output file is created.

Notes:
Calling this in offline mode results in the API just returning without performing any actions or returning any errors.
This operation is no longer run only on the first DUT for all DUT as part of the effort of enabling parallel Init flow execution. Hence when providing a file name for saving data, please make sure different DUTs provide different file name to avoid unpredictable contentTODO: HdmtApi 1.0 Notes
For IntraDUT tests, this operation is only allowed at package level, IP level operation will fail
remarks.example: C# Code Example var resultList = ExecuteAndSaveTDRToFileAsync(testPinGroupName, testIgnorePinsList, testFilename).ToList();

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool ExecuteAndSaveTDRToFile(const std::string& pinGroupName, const std::vector<std::string>& ignorePins, const std::string& filename, std::vector<TDRCalFailResult>& failList);

member.returns: A collection of TDRCalFailResult for the failing pins. If the list is empty, all pins passed

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
TDR cal measures the propagation time(the amount of time for an electrical signal to travel across a trace on the TIU) of the TIU trace to the DUT so that the tester can make adjustments to timings to be accurate at the DUT. If the list of ignore pins is empty, it will use all the pins that belong to the pin group. Otherwise, it will ignore the pins listed.

Notes:
Calling this in offline mode results in the API just returning without performing any actions or returning any errors.
This operation is no longer run only on the first DUT for all DUT as part of the effort of enabling parallel Init flow execution. Hence when providing a file name for saving data, please make sure different DUTs provide different file name to avoid unpredictable contentTODO: HdmtApi 1.0 Notes
For IntraDUT tests, this operation is only allowed at package level, IP level operation will fail
remarks.example: C# Code Example var resultList = ExecuteTDRCalAsync(testPinGroupName, testIgnorePinsList).ToList();
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool ExecuteTDRCal(const std::string& pinGroupName, const std::vector<std::string>& ignorePins, std::vector<TDRCalFailResult>& failList)

Returns collection of TDR Cal data (tiuTdrDelay)

Notes:
Calling this in offline mode results in the API just returning without performing any actions or returning any errors.
This operation is no longer run only on the first DUT for all DUT as part of the effort of enabling parallel Init flow execution. Therefore the data received on each DUT will only contains data for that data, not for all DUTs

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.ReadI2cDataAsync(System.String,System.Byte,System.Int32)
member.summary: Perform an I2C read from an configured I2C pin.
member.param: Pin name of the I2C configured channel to communicate with
I2C address (7-bit address and read bit)
Number of bytes to read from the I2C device

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Reads data from an I2C device connected to an I2C / GPIO pin. See instrument .dat files for instruments that implement the I2C ResourceType.

Notes:
The pin name passed into this function must be a configured I2C channel.

Restrictions:
Read count is limited to 255 bytes.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var readDataList = await wrappedSdkTester.ReadI2cDataAsync(testPinName, testDeviceAddress, testReadCount).ToList();
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool ReadI2cData(const std::string& pinName, unsigned char deviceAddress, int readCount, std::vector<unsigned char>& readData);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.ReadTiuAuxiliaryDataAsync(System.UInt32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the key/value pairs stored in the TIU EEPROM Auxiliary Fields.
member.returns: The returned TIU auxiliary data, in a collection 0 pairs long

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Returns the key/value pairs stored in the TIU EEPROM. Typically these will be a name and a count.

Notes:
The API will throw an exception if the TIU is not docked to the tester, the EEPROM does not exist, or does not contain an extended BLT record.
The Python implementation differs from the C++. The output is returned as the 2nd element in a list instead of updated in place.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var auxiliaryData = await wrappedSdkTester.ReadTiuAuxiliaryDataAsync(testEEPROMIndex, testTiuIndex).ToList();
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool ReadTiuAuxiliaryData(const unsigned int EEPROMIndex, std::vector<AuxiliaryTiuData>& auxiliaryData, int tiuIndex = -1);

member.returns: The counter value to be read from the specified DUT on the given EEPROM

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Reads counter value in TIU EEPROM record for the specified EEPROMSiteIndex and CounterValue is updated in place

Notes:
The API will throw an exception if the EEPROM is not available to the TIU.
In python, the counter value is returned as the second item in a tuple.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var counterValue = await wrappedSdkTester.ReadTIUBoardCounterFromEEPROMAsync(testEEPROMIndex, testCounterIndex, testEEPROMSiteIndex, testTiuIndex);

member.returns: The user data on TIU EEPROM record. It will be a collection of size 128.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Read the user data (initialized to 128 bytes) in TIU EEPROM record with the index (0-27). EEPROM Index should be in the range of 0-7.

Notes:
The API will throw an exception if the TIU is not docked to the tester.
The Python implementation differs from the C++. The input userData is returned as the 4th element in a list instead of updated in place.

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool ReadUserTIUDataFromEEPROM(const unsigned int EEPROMIndex, const unsigned int UserRecordIndex, std::vector<unsigned char>& userData, int tiuIndex = -1);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.WriteI2cDataAsync(System.String,System.Byte,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Byte},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Perform an I2C write from an configured I2C pin.
member.param: Pin name of the I2C configured channel to communicate with
I2C address (7-bit address and write bit)
Byte data to write to device
If true send stop bit after I2C transaction, otherwise do not send stop bit and maintain control of I2C bus

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Writes data to an I2C device connected to an I2C / GPIO pin. After all data is written to the device, a stop bit is written to the I2C bus. See instrument .dat files for instruments that implement the I2C ResourceType.

Notes:
The pin name passed into this function must be a configured I2C channel.

Restrictions:
Write size is limited to 1023 bytes.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkTester.WriteI2cDataAsync(testPinName, testDeviceAddress, testWriteData, testStop);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool WriteI2cData(const std::string& pinName, unsigned char deviceAddress, const std::vector<unsigned char>& writeData, bool stop = true);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTester.WriteReadI2cDataAsync(System.String,System.Byte,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Byte},System.Int32)
member.summary: Perform an I2C write followed by a read from an configured I2C pin without sending a stop bit between each transaction.
member.param: Pin name of the I2C configured channel to communicate with
I2C address of the device (7-bit address, and ignored R/W type bit)
Byte data to write to device
Byte data returned from the device
member.returns: Byte data returned from the device

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Writes data to an I2C device connected to an I2C / GPIO pin without sending a stop condition (stop bit), then performs a read immediately after. This is equivalent to making a call to WriteI2cData with the stop argument set to false followed by a read with ReadI2cData. The least significant bit that determines I2C I/O type is ignored. See instrument .dat files for instruments that implement the I2C ResourceType.

Notes:
The pin name passed into this function must be a configured I2C channel.

Restrictions:
Write size is limited to 1023 bytes.
Read count is limited to 255 bytes.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var readData = await wrappedSdkTester.WriteReadI2cDataAsync(testPinName, testDeviceAddress, testWriteData, testReadCount).ToList();

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool WriteReadI2cData(const std::string& pinName, unsigned char deviceAddress, const std::vector<unsigned char>& writeData, int readCount, std::vector<unsigned char>& readData);

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature bool WriteTiuAuxiliaryData(const unsigned int EEPROMIndex, const std::vector<AuxiliaryTiuData>& auxiliaryData, int tiuIndex = -1);

member.summary: Set the user variable value for the given collection and variable name.
member.param: name of the user variable collection.For module, module name should be prepended to collection name. e.g. moduleName::userVariableCollection
type of user variable (Bool, Int, Double, String, Current, Voltage, Time)

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Set the user variable value for the given collection and variable name. A new user variable will be created and added to the collection if it does not exist.

Notes: Valid values required:
userVariableCollection must be a valid collection name.
userVariableName must be a valid user variable name.
userVariableType must match (or be able to be converted to) the type of userVariableValue.

Restrictions:
TODO: HdmtApi 1.0 Restrictions

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestPlan.GetEnvironmentVariableValueAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the value of the provided environment variable.
member.returns:
Value of the Environment Variable
Example of fixed environment variable: ~HDMT_TPL_DIR and example of environment variable in .env file: HDST_PLIST_PATH.
hdmtOS handles the environment variable names in a case insensitive manner. varx is the same as VARX or VarX.
hdmtOS treats any environment variable with embedded semicolon (;) as a one which is used to specify a search path.
TOS will strip multiple/redundant semicolon (;) in the resolved environment variable along with any trailing or leading white space at the end or in front of a path.

remarks.para:
Notes:
Environment Variable Name must be a valid environment variable name.

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestPlan.GetAllTestConditionNamesAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Returns all defined test condition names of the given type.
member.param: type of test condition to return. One of TIMING, TIMINGS, LEVEL, LEVELS, TRIGGER, ALL, PREBUILT, THERMALMEASURE, THERMALCONTROL.
member.returns: output vector of strings with all test condition names of the given type.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Returns all defined test condition names of the given type in the test program.

Notes: Type must be one of :
PREBUILT : returns all test conditions built by HdmtApi
ALL : returns all non-pre-built test conditions
TIMING or TIMINGS : returns all the timings test conditions
LEVEL or LEVELS : returns all the levels test conditions
THERMALMEASURE : returns all the thermal measure test conditions
THERMALCONTROL : returns all the thermal control set test conditions
TRIGGER : returns all the trigger test conditions
remarks.example: C# Code Example var testConditionsNamesResult = await wrappedSdkTestPlan.GetAllTestConditionNamesAsync(testConditionType);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetAllTestConditionNames(const std::string& testConditionType, std::vector<std::string>& outputAllTestConditionNames);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestPlan.GetLoopingStatusAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Gets specified flow item current loop execution status, such as current loop value and total loop value.
member.param: Name of the flow item to be queried for looping status.
member.returns: Get flow item current loop execution status.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
This API gets specified flow item current loop execution status.
CompletedTimeInSeconds indicates the time elapsed from the beginning of the loop to the time this API is called.
CompletedLoopIterations indicates the number of loop that has been completely finished.
TotalLoopValue indicates the loop value user set.
- For LoopCount looping type, it is the total number of loop count.
- For MinDuration looping type, it is the minimum execution time.

Notes:
When the specified flow item is not executing at the time this API is called, both CompletedTimeInSeconds and CompletedLoopIterations will be the same as TotalLoopValue.
remarks.example: C# Code Example var status = await wrappedSdkTestPlan.GetLoopingStatusAsync(flowItemName);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetLoopingStatus(const std::string& flowItemName, HDMT::LoopStatus& status);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestPlan.GetMultiTrialTestContentAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Get specified MultiTrialTest content.

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
This API returns specified MultiTrialTest content.

Restrictions:
The specified MultiTrialTest must exist, otherwise this API will return false.

Notes:
The MultiTrialTest name can be obtained through a flow iterator which returns true on IsMultiTrialFlow() call.

C++ Original HdmtApi Signature GetMultiTrialTestContent(const std::string& mttName, MultiTrialTestContent& content);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestPlan.RegisterFlowEventCallbackAsync(HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IFlowEvent)
member.summary: Registers a callback object with functions that will be invoked when certain pre-defined flows begin and end

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Registers a callback object with functions that will be invoked when certain pre-defined flows begin and end. The registration is per-DUT context, and only one callback object can be registered for each DUT context. Subsequent calls to this API will replace the previously registered callback object. The FlowBeginEvent and FlowEndEvent functions on the callback object will be invoked for the following defined flows: - InitFlow - LotStartFlow - LotEndFlow - MainFlow - TestPlanStartFlow - TestPlanEndFlow - PreDefinedFlow1-12 If either the FlowBeginEvent or FlowEndEvent callback functions return false, the flow will exit out of the port returned by the callback function. If an error message is returned, it will be logged. For HVM flows, the TestPlanEndFlow will still be run and a TOS-defined bin of 9802 will be set. If the FlowBeginEvent callback function returns false, the flow items in the flow and the FlowEndEvent function will not be executed.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkTestPlan.RegisterFlowEventCallbackAsync(callback);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature RegisterFlowEventCallback(std::shared_ptr<HDMT::IFlowEvent> callback);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestPlan.RegisterGuiCallbackAsync(HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IGuiCallback)
member.summary: Registers a callback object with functions that will be invoked by the hdmtOS GUI

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Registers a callback object with functions that will be invoked when the following events occur: - When a user executes a shmoo that involves a call back user function dimension The registration is per-DUT context, and only one callback object can be registered for each DUT context. Subsequent calls to this API will replace the previously registered callback object.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkTestPlan.RegisterGuiCallbackAsync(callback);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature RegisterGuiCallback(std::shared_ptr<HDMT::IGuiCallback> callback);

member.name: M:HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.IWrappedSdkTestPlan.SetConcurrentFlowExecutionModeAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},HdmtOs.Sdk.Wrapped.ConcurrentFlowExecutionMode)
member.summary: Sets execution mode for provided concurrent flows, or all concurrent flows if none provided
member.param: Concurrent flows to change execution mode, or empty for all

remarks.para:
Detailed Description:
Sets execution mode for provided concurrent flows. When the flow name list is empty, it will apply the mode to all concurrent flows; otherwise it will only affect the specified flows. If user ever provide any flow name, it must be a concurrent flow and it must exist; otherwise this API returns false and none of the concurrent flows will be changed. If user doesn't provide a flow name, and there are no concurrent flows defined, this API will return true without doing anything.

Restrictions:
1. Although this API can be called in any flow, it is highly recommended to call it as part of Init flow.

Notes:
1. When calling this API on an IP DUT, since IP DUTs never have concurrent flow it will do nothing if the user provided an empty list and return false if the user provides any flow names.
remarks.example: C# Code Example await wrappedSdkTestPlan.SetConcurrentFlowExecutionModeAsync(flowNames, mode);
C++ Original HdmtApi Signature SetConcurrentFlowExecutionMode(const std::vector<std::string>& flowNames, HdmtApi::ConcurrentFlowExecutionMode mode);
